description: §52 multiple independent server-fn effects drain in ONE settle. Two server fns (loadA, loadB) feed two cells (@a, @b). Two clicks fire both async server calls; a single wait:"settle" drains BOTH pending server-fn await chains (the macrotask boundary empties the whole microtask queue first). After settle @a has 2 rows and @b has 1, so #counts reads "A:2 B:1". Proves settled() cleanly drains a multi-server-fn sequence.
rationale: §52 each server-fn call round-trips independently through its own compiler-emitted route; the two reactive writes (@a, @b) both complete before the snapshot because settle drains every pending continuation. The #counts interpolation reads both lengths.
